mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns within a 5-day window.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative or flat 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous industrial production expansion and housing starts contraction signal an economy overheating in manufacturing while residential investment stalls, creating inflationary supply bottlenecks that force equity valuations lower over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in over 60 percent of cases where INDPRO rises and HOUST falls concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: During active federal funds rate hikes, any month-over-month increase in the CPI index triggers a risk-off repricing as markets anticipate further tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of triggered instances during rate hike months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales and food services revenues directly reflect accelerating consumer demand, which translates into higher near-term corporate earnings guidance and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS month-over-month changes and 5-day SPX returns falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in industrial production exceeding 1.5 percent signals a contraction in manufacturing output and capacity utilization, reducing corporate profit margins and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day period.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60 percent of quarters where INDPRO declines exceed 1.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent nonfarm payroll growth exceeding 150,000 and a declining unemployment rate confirm a self-reinforcing labor demand expansion, which sustains consumer spending power and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative in over 55 percent of months where PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing market exceeding 1,900 thousand annual starts, any acceleration in real GDP growth amplifies construction-related demand and wealth effects, producing strongly positive S&P 500 forward returns within a 5-day trading window.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are flat or negative in more than 50 percent of instances where HOUST exceeds 1900 and GDPC1 accelerates.
